title: Pithecellobium trapezifolium
intro: Pithecellobium trapezifolium, also known as the "Guayacán" tree, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pithecellobium trapezifolium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the Guayacán tree is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Pithecellobium trapezifolium is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to the Americas, specifically in tropical regions. This plant is classified as a member of the genus Pithecellobium, which is a group of tropical trees and shrubs. The genus Pithecellobium is further classified under the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Pithecellobium trapezifolium shares characteristics with other legumes.
uses: Pithecellobium trapezifolium is a plant species native to Central and South America, and its bark is traditionally used in folk medicine.
